title: DatasetManifest
description: Provenance record for a dataset used by the lab. Every source is free, public, and key-less ($0 cloud spend). The manifest pins the source, the covered period, the symbols, a content hash for reproducibility, the KNOWN BIASES (survivorship, look-ahead, regime, etc.) the data carries, and the rate limits of the public endpoint — so a result can be reproduced and its data caveats are never silent.

datasetId.description: Stable identifier for this dataset artifact.

source.description: Where the data came from (a free, public, key-less source).

provider.description: Provider name, e.g. Binance, Coin Metrics Community.

endpoint.description: Public host / API endpoint used (no API key).

period.description: Inclusive coverage window of the dataset.

hash.description: Content hash of the materialized dataset (e.g. sha256:...), for reproducibility.

known_biases.description: Known biases this data carries. At least one entry is required (use 'none_known' explicitly if truly none) so caveats are never silent.

rate_limits.description: Rate limits of the public endpoint, so re-fetches stay within policy.

requestsPerMinute.description: Allowed requests per minute against the endpoint.

weightPerMinute.description: Optional weight-based budget per minute (e.g. Binance request weight).

notes.description: Free-text notes about throttling / backoff behavior.
